yamlutil\n6\t\n7\timport (\n8\t\t\"fmt\"\n9\t\t\"strings\"\n10\t)\n11\t\n12\t// ParseErrorKind represents the category of parse error.\n13\t// This serves as an enum-style discriminator for different error types.\n14\ttype ParseErrorKind string\n15\t\n16\tconst (\n17\t\t// ParseErrorKindSyntax indicates YAML syntax errors (indentation, malformed YAML, etc.)\n18\t\tParseErrorKindSyntax ParseErrorKind = \"syntax\"\n19\t\n20\t\t// ParseErrorKindStructure indicates YAML structure errors (duplicate keys, invalid nesting, etc.)\n21\t\tParseErrorKindStructure ParseErrorKind = \"structure\"\n22\t\n23\t\t// ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch indicates type conversion errors (string to int, etc.)\n24\t\tParseErrorKindTypeMismatch ParseErrorKind = \"type mismatch\"\n25\t\n26\t\t// ParseErrorKindIO indicates file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.)\n27\t\tParseErrorKindIO ParseErrorKind = \"io\"\n28\t\n29\t\t// ParseErrorKindValidation indicates semantic validation errors (required fields, constraints, etc.)\n30\t\tParseErrorKindValidation ParseErrorKind = \"validation\"\n31\t\n32\t\t// ParseErrorKindSchema indicates schema validation errors\n33\t\tParseErrorKindSchema ParseErrorKind = \"schema\"\n34\t\n35\t\t// ParseErrorKindEmpty indicates empty file or content\n36\t\tParseErrorKindEmpty ParseErrorKind = \"empty\"\n37\t\n38\t\t// ParseErrorKindUnknown indicates an unknown error type\n39\t\tParseErrorKindUnknown ParseErrorKind = \"unknown\"\n40\t)\n41\t\n42\t// ParseErrorDetail contains specific details for different error kinds.\n43\t// Only relevant fields are populated based on the ErrorKind.\n44\ttype ParseErrorDetail struct {\n45\t\t// Syntax-specific fields\n46\t\tExpected string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n47\t\tFound    string // What was actually found\n48\t\n49\t\t// Structure-specific fields\n50\t\tDuplicateKey string // Name of duplicate key (for structure errors)\n51\t\tLocation     string // Nested path to error location\n52\t\n53\t\t// Type mismatch-specific fields\n54\t\tFieldPath    string // Dot-notation path to field (for type errors)\n55\t\tExpectedType string // Expected type\n56\t\tActualType   string // Actual type found\n57\t\tValue        string // Actual value that caused error\n58\t\n59\t\t// Validation-specific fields\n60\t\tConstraintType string // Type of constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n61\t\tConstraint     string // Description of constraint\n62\t\n63\t\t// Schema-specific fields\n64\t\tSchemaPath string // Path to schema file\n65\t\tSchemaName string // Name of schema\n66\t}\n67\t\n68\t// ErrorContext provides rich context about the error location.\n69\ttype ErrorContext struct {\n70\t\t// Line is the line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n71\t\tLine int\n72\t\n73\t\t// Column is the column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n74\t\tColumn int\n75\t\n76\t\t// Snippet is the line of source code where error occurred\n77\t\tSnippet string\n78\t\n79\t\t// SurroundingLines provides context around the error line\n80\t\tSurroundingLines []string\n81\t\n82\t\t// SnippetLineIndex is the index of the error line within SurroundingLines\n83\t\tSnippetLineIndex int\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// EnhancedParseError provides comprehensive error information for parsing failures.\n87\t//\n88\t// This enhanced error type includes:\n89\t// - Error kind categorization (syntax, structure, type mismatch, etc.)\n90\t// - Detailed error-specific information\n91\t// - Rich context including source snippets\n92\t// - Integration with Result<T, ParseError> pattern\n93\ttype EnhancedParseError struct {\n94\t\t// Kind categorizes the error type\n95\t\tKind ParseErrorKind\n96\t\n97\t\t// FilePath is the path to the file being parsed\n98\t\tFilePath string\n99\t\n100\t\t// Message is a human-readable error message\n101\t\tMessage string\n102\t\n103\t\t// Context provides location and source context information\n104\t\tLocationInfo ErrorContext\n105\t\n106\t\t// Detail contains error-specific details\n107\t\tDetail ParseErrorDetail\n108\t\n109\t\t// UnderlyingErr is the wrapped error for compatibility\n110\t\tUnderlyingErr error\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t// Error implements the error interface.\n114\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Error() string {\n115\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n116\t\n117\t\t// Build error header\n118\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n119\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s at line %d\", e.Kind, e.FilePath, e.LocationInfo.Line))\n120\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n121\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n122\t\t\t}\n123\t\t} else {\n124\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s\", e.Kind, e.FilePath))\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\t// Add kind-specific details\n128\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n129\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n130\t\t\t// For type mismatch, if we have field info, format it specially\n131\t\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n132\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", field %s: expected %s, got: %s\",\n133\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n134\t\t\t\t// Add parenthetical with full details (note: uses \"got:\" for consistency)\n135\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, expected: %s, got: %s)\",\n136\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n137\t\t\t} else if e.Message != \"\" {\n138\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n139\t\t\t}\n140\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n141\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n142\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n143\t\t\t}\n144\t\t\tif e.Detail.Expected != \"\" || e.Detail.Found != \"\" {\n145\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", e.Detail.Expected, e.Detail.Found))\n146\t\t\t}\n147\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n148\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n149\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n150\t\t\t}\n151\t\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n152\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (duplicate key: %s\", e.Detail.DuplicateKey))\n153\t\t\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n154\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n155\t\t\t\t}\n156\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n157\t\t\t}\n158\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n159\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n160\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n161\t\t\t}\n162\t\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" && e.Detail.Constraint != \"\" {\n163\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, constraint: %s)\",\n164\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.Constraint))\n165\t\t\t}\n166\t\tdefault:\n167\t\t\t// For other error types, just add the message\n168\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n169\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n170\t\t\t}\n171\t\t}\n172\t\n173\t\treturn sb.String()\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n177\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Unwrap() error {\n178\t\treturn e.UnderlyingErr\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\n182\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n183\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n184\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n185\t\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n186\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n187\t\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n188\t\t\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n189\t\t\t}\n190\t\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n191\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n192\t\t\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n193\t\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n194\t\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n195\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n196\t\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n197\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n198\t\t\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n199\t\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n200\t\t\treturn ErrCodeFileEmpty\n201\t\tdefault:\n202\t\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n203\t\t}\n204\t}\n205\t\n206\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n207\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n208\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n209\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n210\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n211\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n212\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n213\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n214\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n215\t\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n216\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeIO\n217\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n218\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n219\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n220\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeSchema\n221\t\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n222\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeEmpty\n223\t\tdefault:\n224\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n225\t\t}\n226\t}\n227\t\n228\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n229\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Context() string {\n230\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n231\t\n232\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n233\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"line: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Line))\n234\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n235\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n236\t\t\t}\n237\t\t}\n238\t\n239\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n240\t\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n241\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n242\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s\", e.Detail.FieldPath))\n244\t\t}\n245\t\n246\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n247\t\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n248\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n249\t\t\t}\n250\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n251\t\t}\n252\t\n253\t\treturn sb.String()\n254\t}\n255\t\n256\t// String returns a detailed multi-line error representation with source snippet.\n257\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) String() string {\n258\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n259\t\n260\t\t// Error header\n261\t\tsb.WriteString(e.Error())\n262\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n263\t\n264\t\t// Show snippet with caret pointer if we have it\n265\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n266\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n267\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n268\t\t\tsb.WriteString(e.LocationInfo.Snippet)\n269\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n270\t\n271\t\t\t// Add column indicator if available\n272\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 && e.LocationInfo.Column <= len(e.LocationInfo.Snippet) {\n273\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n274\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < e.LocationInfo.Column-1; i++ {\n275\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" \")\n276\t\t\t\t}\n277\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"^--- here\\n\")\n278\t\t\t}\n279\t\t}\n280\t\n281\t\t// Add surrounding context if available\n282\t\tif len(e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines) > 0 {\n283\t\t\t// Add blank line separator if we showed the snippet\n284\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n285\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n286\t\t\t}\n287\t\t\tfor i, line := range e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines {\n288\t\t\t\tif i == e.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex {\n289\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"> \")\n290\t\t\t\t} else {\n291\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n292\t\t\t\t}\n293\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(line)\n294\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n295\t\t\t}\n296\t\t}\n297\t\n298\t\treturn sb.String()\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t// IsSyntaxError checks if this is a syntax error.\n302\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSyntaxError() bool {\n303\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSyntax\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// IsStructureError checks if this is a structure error.\n307\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsStructureError() bool {\n308\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindStructure\n309\t}\n310\t\n311\t// IsTypeMismatchError checks if this is a type mismatch error.\n312\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsTypeMismatchError() bool {\n313\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t// IsIOError checks if this is an I/O error.\n317\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsIOError() bool {\n318\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindIO\n319\t}\n320\t\n321\t// IsValidationError checks if this is a validation error.\n322\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsValidationError() bool {\n323\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindValidation\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t// IsSchemaError checks if this is a schema error.\n327\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSchemaError() bool {\n328\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSchema\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// IsEmpty checks if this is an empty file error.\n332\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsEmpty() bool {\n333\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindEmpty\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\t// ============================================================================\n337\t// Error Construction Helpers\n338\t// ============================================================================\n339\t\n340\t// NewSyntaxParseError creates a new syntax error.\n341\tfunc NewSyntaxParseError(filePath, message string, message,\n346\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n347\t\t\t\tLine:   line,\n348\t\t\t\tColumn: column,\n349\t\t\t},\n350\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n351\t\t\t\tExpected: expected,\n352\t\t\t\tFound:    found,\n353\t\t\t},\n354\t\t}\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// NewStructureParseError creates a new structure error.\n358\tfunc NewStructureParseError(filePath, message string, line int, duplicateKey, location string) *EnhancedParseError {\n359\t\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n360\t\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindStructure,\n361\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n362\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n363\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n364\t\t\t\tLine: line,\n365\t\t\t},\n366\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n367\t\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n368\t\t\t\tLocation: filePath,\n378\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n379\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n380\t\t\t\tLine: line,\n381\t\t\t},\n382\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n383\t\t\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n384\t\t\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n385\t\t\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n386\t\t\t\tValue:        value,\n387\t\t\t},\n388\t\t}\n389\t}\n390\t\n391\t// NewIOParseError creates a new ============================================================================\n447\t// Error Propagation Strategy\n448\t// ============================================================================\n449\t\n450\t// The error propagation strategy for ParseError follows these principles:\n451\t//\n452\t// 1. Always provide rich context: Include line numbers, column positions, and\n453\t//    source snippets whenever possible.\n454\t//\n455\t// 2. Preserve error chain: Use error wrapping (errors.Wrap, fmt.Errorf with %w)\n456\t//    to maintain the full error chain for debugging.\n457\t//\n458\t// 3. Type-safe error handling: Use Result[T, ParseError] pattern for compile-time\n459\t//    error handling guarantees.\n460\t//\n461\t// 4. Error transformation: Transform lower-level errors (e.g., yaml.v3 errors)\n462\t//    into EnhancedParseError with appropriate context and snippets.\n463\t//\n464\t// 5. Fail-fast on critical errors: I/O errors and syntax errors should immediately\n465\t//    return errors without attempting further processing.\n466\t//\n467\t// Error Flow:\n468\t//   Raw Error → EnhancedParseError (with context) → Result[T, ParseError]\n469\t//\n470\t// Example propagation:\n471\t//   yaml.Unmarshal error → extract line/column → read source snippet →\n472\t//   NewSyntaxParseError with snippet → Result[T, ParseError]\n473\t\n474\t// ============================================================================\n475\t// Integration with Legacy Error Types\n476\t// ============================================================================\n477\t\n478\t// ToLegacySyntaxError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy SyntaxError.\n479\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacySyntaxError() *SyntaxError {\n480\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindSyntax {\n481\t\t\treturn nil\n482\t\t}\n483\t\treturn &SyntaxError{\n484\t\t\tFilePath:  e.FilePath,\n485\t\t\tLine:      e.LocationInfo.Line,\n486\t\t\tColumn:    e.LocationInfo.Column,\n487\t\t\tMessage:   e.Message,\n488\t\t\tExpected:  e.Detail.Expected,\n489\t\t\tFound:     e.Detail.Found,\n490\t\t\tErr:       e.UnderlyingErr,\n491\t\t\tErrorCode: e.Code(),\n492\t\t}\n493\t}\n494\t\n495\t// ToLegacyStructureError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy StructureError.\n496\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyStructureError() *StructureError {\n497\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindStructure {\n498\t\t\treturn nil\n499\t\t}\n500\t\treturn &StructureError{\n501\t\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n502\t\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n503\t\t\tMessage:      e.Message,\n504\t\t\tDuplicateKey: e.Detail.DuplicateKey,\n505\t\t\tLocation:     e.Detail.Location,\n506\t\t\tErr:          e.UnderlyingErr,\n507\t\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n508\t\t}\n509\t}\n510\t\n511\t// ToLegacyTypeMismatchError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy TypeMismatchError.\n512\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyTypeMismatchError() *TypeMismatchError {\n513\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch {\n514\t\t\treturn nil\n515\t\t}\n516\t\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n517\t\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n518\t\t\tFieldPath:    e.Detail.FieldPath,\n519\t\t\tExpectedType: e.Detail.ExpectedType,\n520\t\t\tActualType:   e.Detail.ActualType,\n521\t\t\tValue:        e.Detail.Value,\n522\t\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n523\t\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n524\t\t}\n525\t}\n526\t\n527\t// ToLegacyParseError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy ParseError.\n528\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyParseError() *ParseError {\n529\t\treturn &ParseError{\n530\t\t\tFilePath:   e.FilePath,\n531\t\t\tLine:       e.LocationInfo.Line,\n532\t\t\tColumn:     e.LocationInfo.Column,\n533\t\t\tMessage:    e.Message,\n534\t\t\tContextStr: e.Context(),\n535\t\t\tErr:        e.UnderlyingErr,\n536\t\t\tErrorType:  e.YAMLErrorType(),\n537\t\t\tErrorCode:  e.Code(),\n538\t\t}\n539\t}\n540\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"eb300492-b26b-4e28-861b-84cc576df4b5","uuid":"3128ac21-0bdb-4a90-aab2-fc6f6838fcea","timestamp":"2026-07-11T20:07:14.283Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/parse_error_design.go","content":"// Package yamlutil provides enhanced ParseError type design for failure cases.\n//\n// This file implements the comprehensive ParseError design that integrates\n// with the Result<T, ParseError> pattern for type-safe error handling.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// ParseErrorKind represents the category of parse error.\n// This serves as an enum-style discriminator for different error types.\ntype ParseErrorKind string\n\nconst (\n\t// ParseErrorKindSyntax indicates YAML syntax errors (indentation, malformed YAML, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindSyntax ParseErrorKind = \"syntax\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindStructure indicates YAML structure errors (duplicate keys, invalid nesting, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindStructure ParseErrorKind = \"structure\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch indicates type conversion errors (string to int, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindTypeMismatch ParseErrorKind = \"type mismatch\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindIO indicates file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindIO ParseErrorKind = \"io\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindValidation indicates semantic validation errors (required fields, constraints, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindValidation ParseErrorKind = \"validation\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindSchema indicates schema validation errors\n\tParseErrorKindSchema ParseErrorKind = \"schema\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindEmpty indicates empty file or content\n\tParseErrorKindEmpty ParseErrorKind = \"empty\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindUnknown indicates an unknown error type\n\tParseErrorKindUnknown ParseErrorKind = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// ParseErrorDetail contains specific details for different error kinds.\n// Only relevant fields are populated based on the ErrorKind.\ntype ParseErrorDetail struct {\n\t// Syntax-specific fields\n\tExpected string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n\tFound    string // What was actually found\n\n\t// Structure-specific fields\n\tDuplicateKey string // Name of duplicate key (for structure errors)\n\tLocation     string // Nested path to error location\n\n\t// Type mismatch-specific fields\n\tFieldPath    string // Dot-notation path to field (for type errors)\n\tExpectedType string // Expected type\n\tActualType   string // Actual type found\n\tValue        string // Actual value that caused error\n\n\t// Validation-specific fields\n\tConstraintType string // Type of constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n\tConstraint     string // Description of constraint\n\n\t// Schema-specific fields\n\tSchemaPath string // Path to schema file\n\tSchemaName string // Name of schema\n}\n\n// ErrorContext provides rich context about the error location.\ntype ErrorContext struct {\n\t// Line is the line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tLine int\n\n\t// Column is the column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn int\n\n\t// Snippet is the line of source code where error occurred\n\tSnippet string\n\n\t// SurroundingLines provides context around the error line\n\tSurroundingLines []string\n\n\t// SnippetLineIndex is the index of the error line within SurroundingLines\n\tSnippetLineIndex int\n}\n\n// EnhancedParseError provides comprehensive error information for parsing failures.\n//\n// This enhanced error type includes:\n// - Error kind categorization (syntax, structure, type mismatch, etc.)\n// - Detailed error-specific information\n// - Rich context including source snippets\n// - Integration with Result<T, ParseError> pattern\ntype EnhancedParseError struct {\n\t// Kind categorizes the error type\n\tKind ParseErrorKind\n\n\t// FilePath is the path to the file being parsed\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Message is a human-readable error message\n\tMessage string\n\n\t// Context provides location and source context information\n\tLocationInfo ErrorContext\n\n\t// Detail contains error-specific details\n\tDetail ParseErrorDetail\n\n\t// UnderlyingErr is the wrapped error for compatibility\n\tUnderlyingErr error\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build error header\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s at line %d\", e.Kind, e.FilePath, e.LocationInfo.Line))\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s\", e.Kind, e.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add kind-specific details\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\t// For type mismatch, if we have field info, format it specially\n\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", field %s: expected %s, got: %s\",\n\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n\t\t\t// Add parenthetical with full details (note: uses \"got:\" for consistency)\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, expected: %s, got: %s)\",\n\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n\t\t} else if e.Message != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif e.Detail.Expected != \"\" || e.Detail.Found != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", e.Detail.Expected, e.Detail.Found))\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (duplicate key: %s\", e.Detail.DuplicateKey))\n\t\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" && e.Detail.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, constraint: %s)\",\n\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.Constraint))\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// For other error types, just add the message\n\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn e.UnderlyingErr\n}\n\n// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeFileEmpty\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeIO\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSchema\n\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeEmpty\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n\t}\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Context() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"line: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Line))\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s\", e.Detail.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// String returns a detailed multi-line error representation with source snippet.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Error header\n\tsb.WriteString(e.Error())\n\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\n\t// Show snippet with caret pointer if we have it\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n\t\tsb.WriteString(e.LocationInfo.Snippet)\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\n\t\t// Add column indicator if available\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 && e.LocationInfo.Column <= len(e.LocationInfo.Snippet) {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < e.LocationInfo.Column-1; i++ {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" \")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"^--- here\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Add surrounding context if available\n\tif len(e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines) > 0 {\n\t\t// Add blank line separator if we showed the snippet\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor i, line := range e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines {\n\t\t\tif i == e.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"> \")\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(line)\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// IsSyntaxError checks if this is a syntax error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSyntaxError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSyntax\n}\n\n// IsStructureError checks if this is a structure error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsStructureError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindStructure\n}\n\n// IsTypeMismatchError checks if this is a type mismatch error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsTypeMismatchError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// IsIOError checks if this is an I/O error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsIOError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindIO\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if this is a validation error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsValidationError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindValidation\n}\n\n// IsSchemaError checks if this is a schema error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSchemaError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSchema\n}\n\n// IsEmpty checks if this is an empty file error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsEmpty() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindEmpty\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Error Construction Helpers\n// ============================================================================\n\n// NewSyntaxParseError creates a new syntax error.\nfunc NewSyntaxParseError(filePath, message string, line, column int, expected, found string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSyntax,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine:   line,\n\t\t\tColumn: column,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tExpected: expected,\n\t\t\tFound:    found,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewStructureParseError creates a new structure error.\nfunc NewStructureParseError(filePath, message string, line int, duplicateKey, location string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindStructure,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewTypeMismatchParseError creates a new type mismatch error.\nfunc NewTypeMismatchParseError(filePath, message string, line int, fieldPath, expectedType, actualType, value string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n\t\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n\t\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n\t\t\tValue:        value,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewIOParseError creates a new I/O error.\nfunc NewIOParseError(filePath, message string, line int, underlyingErr error) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:          ParseErrorKindIO,\n\t\tFilePath:      filePath,\n\t\tMessage:       message,\n\t\tUnderlyingErr: underlyingErr,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewValidationParseError creates a new validation error.\nfunc NewValidationParseError(filePath, message string, line int, fieldPath, constraintType, constraint string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindValidation,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:      fieldPath,\n\t\t\tConstraintType: constraintType,\n\t\t\tConstraint:     constraint,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaParseError creates a new schema error.\nfunc NewSchemaParseError(filePath, message string, line int, schemaPath, schemaName string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSchema,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tSchemaPath: schemaPath,\n\t\t\tSchemaName: schemaName,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewEmptyParseError creates a new empty file error.\nfunc NewEmptyParseError(filePath string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindEmpty,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  \"file is empty or contains only whitespace\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Error Propagation Strategy\n// ============================================================================\n\n// The error propagation strategy for ParseError follows these principles:\n//\n// 1. Always provide rich context: Include line numbers, column positions, and\n//    source snippets whenever possible.\n//\n// 2. Preserve error chain: Use error wrapping (errors.Wrap, fmt.Errorf with %w)\n//    to maintain the full error chain for debugging.\n//\n// 3. Type-safe error handling: Use Result[T, ParseError] pattern for compile-time\n//    error handling guarantees.\n//\n// 4. Error transformation: Transform lower-level errors (e.g., yaml.v3 errors)\n//    into EnhancedParseError with appropriate context and snippets.\n//\n// 5. Fail-fast on critical errors: I/O errors and syntax errors should immediately\n//    return errors without attempting further processing.\n//\n// Error Flow:\n//   Raw Error → EnhancedParseError (with context) → Result[T, ParseError]\n//\n// Example propagation:\n//   yaml.Unmarshal error → extract line/column → read source snippet →\n//   NewSyntaxParseError with snippet → Result[T, ParseError]\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Integration with Legacy Error Types\n// ============================================================================\n\n// ToLegacySyntaxError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy SyntaxError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacySyntaxError() *SyntaxError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindSyntax {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &SyntaxError{\n\t\tFilePath:  e.FilePath,\n\t\tLine:      e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tColumn:    e.LocationInfo.Column,\n\t\tMessage:   e.Message,\n\t\tExpected:  e.Detail.Expected,\n\t\tFound:     e.Detail.Found,\n\t\tErr:       e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorCode: e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyStructureError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy StructureError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyStructureError() *StructureError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindStructure {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &StructureError{\n\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tMessage:      e.Message,\n\t\tDuplicateKey: e.Detail.DuplicateKey,\n\t\tLocation:     e.Detail.Location,\n\t\tErr:          e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyTypeMismatchError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy TypeMismatchError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyTypeMismatchError() *TypeMismatchError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:    e.Detail.FieldPath,\n\t\tExpectedType: e.Detail.ExpectedType,\n\t\tActualType:   e.Detail.ActualType,\n\t\tValue:        e.Detail.Value,\n\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyParseError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy ParseError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyParseError() *ParseError {\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tFilePath:   e.FilePath,\n\t\tLine:       e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tColumn:     e.LocationInfo.Column,\n\t\tMessage:    e.Message,\n\t\tContextStr: e.Context(),\n\t\tErr:        e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorType:  e.YAMLErrorType(),\n\t\tErrorCode:  e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n","numLines":540,"startLine":1,"totalLines":540}}}

handling and user-friendly error messages.\n69\t// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\n70\ttype ParseErrorVariant string\n71\t\n72\tconst (\n73\t\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n74\t\t//\n75\t\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n76\t\t// - Invalid indentation\n77\t\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n78\t\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n79\t\t// - Improper quoting\n80\t\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n81\t\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n82\t\n83\t\t// pe.Column))\n235\t\t\t}\n236\t\t} else {\n237\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n238\t\t}\n239\t\n240\t\t// Add message\n241\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n242\t\n243\t\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n244\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n245\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n246\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n247\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n248\t\t\t}\n249\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n250\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n251\t\t\t}\n252\t\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n253\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n254\t\t\t}\n255\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n256\t\t}\n257\t\n258\t\treturn sb.String()\n259\t}\n260\t\n261\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n262\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n263\t\treturn pe.Err\n264\t}\n265\t\n266\t// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n267\t//\n268\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n269\t// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n270\t//\n271\t// Parameters:\n272\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n273\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n274\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n275\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n276\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n277\t//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n278\t//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n279\t//\n280\t// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n281\t//\n282\t// Example usage:\n283\t//\n284\t//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\n285\tfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n286\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n287\t\terrorCode := code\n288\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n289\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n290\t\t}\n291\t\n292\t\treturn &ParseError{\n293\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n294\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n295\t\t\tLine:      line,\n296\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n297\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n298\t\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n299\t\t\tExpected:  expected,\n300\t\t\tActual:    actual,\n301\t\t}\n302\t}\n303\t\n304\t// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\n305\tfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n306\t\tvar pe *ParseError\n307\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n308\t}\n309\t\n310\t// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n311\t//\n312\t// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n313\t// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n314\t// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\n315\ttype ValidationError struct {\n316\t\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n317\t\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n318\t\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n319\t\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n320\t\tColumn     int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n321\t\tConstraint string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n322\t\tContextStr string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n323\t\tErr        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n324\t\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n325\t\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n329\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n330\t\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n331\t\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n332\t\t}\n333\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n337\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n338\t\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n339\t\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n340\t\t\treturn ve.Type\n341\t\t}\n342\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n343\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n344\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n345\t\t}\n346\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n347\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n348\t\t}\n349\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n350\t}\n351\t\n352\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n353\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n354\t\treturn ve.ContextStr\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// Error implements the error interface.\n358\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n359\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n360\t\n361\t\t// Build base error with location\n362\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n363\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n364\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n365\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n366\t\t\t}\n367\t\t} else {\n368\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n369\t\t}\n370\t\n371\t\t// Add field path if available\n372\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n373\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n374\t\t}\n375\t\n376\t\t// Add message\n377\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n378\t\n379\t\t// Add constraint if available\n380\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n381\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n382\t\t}\n383\t\n384\t\treturn sb.String()\n385\t}\n386\t\n387\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n388\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n389\t\treturn ve.Err\n390\t}\n391\t\n392\t// String returns a formatted error message with context.\n393\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n394\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n395\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n396\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n397\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n398\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n399\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n400\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n401\t\t\t}\n402\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n403\t\t}\n404\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n405\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n406\t\t}\n407\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n408\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n409\t\t}\n410\t\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n411\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n412\t\t}\n413\t\treturn sb.String()\n414\t}\n415\t\n416\t// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n417\t//\n418\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n419\t// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, error code, and location.\n420\t//\n421\t// Parameters:\n422\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n423\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n424\t//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n425\t//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n426\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n427\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n428\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n429\t//   - errorType: Category of error (use empty string for default ErrorTypeValidation)\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue, 10, 5, \"\")\n436\tfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode, line int, column int, errorType ErrorType) *ValidationError {\n437\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n438\t\terrorCode := code\n439\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n440\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n441\t\t}\n442\t\n443\t\t// Use provided error type or default to validation error\n444\t\teType := errorType\n445\t\tif eType == \"\" {\n446\t\t\teType = ErrorTypeValidation\n447\t\t}\n448\t\n449\t\treturn &ValidationError{\n450\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n451\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n452\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n453\t\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n454\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n455\t\t\tLine:       line,\n456\t\t\tColumn:     column,\n457\t\t\tType:       eType,\n458\t\t}\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\t// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\n462\tfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n463\t\tvar ve *ValidationError\n464\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n465\t}\n466\t\n467\t// NewFileError creates a new FileError with proper initialization.\n468\tfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n469\t\treturn &FileError{\n470\t\t\tPath:       path,\n471\t\t\tOperation:  operation,\n472\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n473\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n474\t\t}\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper initialization.\n478\tfunc NewSchemaValidationError(filePath string, schemaPath string, fieldPath string, message string, expected string, found string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *SchemaValidationError {\n479\t\treturn &SchemaValidationError{\n480\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n481\t\t\tSchemaPath: schemaPath,\n482\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n483\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n484\t\t\tExpected:   expected,\n485\t\t\tFound:      found,\n486\t\t\tLine:       line,\n487\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n488\t\t}\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t// NewTypeMismatchError creates a new TypeMismatchError with proper    value,\n499\t\t\tLine:         line,\n500\t\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n501\t\t}\n502\t}\n503\t\n504\t// NewFieldNotFoundError creates a new FieldNotFoundError with proper filePath,\n508\t\t\tFieldPath: fieldPath,\n509\t\t\tLine:      line,\n510\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n511\t\t}\n512\t}\n513\t\n514\t// NewConstraintError creates a new ConstraintError with proper constraint,\n521\t\t\tValue:          value,\n522\t\t\tLine:           line,\n523\t\t\tErrorCode:      errorCode,\n524\t\t}\n525\t}\n526\t\n527\t// NewSyntaxError creates a new SyntaxError with proper found,\n536\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n537\t\t}\n538\t}\n539\t\n540\t// NewStructureError creates a new StructureError with proper initialization.\n541\tfunc NewStructureError(filePath string, message,\n545\t\t\tLine:         line,\n546\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n547\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n548\t\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n549\t\t}\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t// NewDuplicateKeyError with the given parameters.\n556\t//\n557\t// Parameters:\n558\t//   - filePath: Path to the file with duplicate keys\n559\t//   - key: The duplicate key name\n560\t//   - location: Nested path to the interface.\n566\tfunc NewDuplicateKeyError(filePath string, key string, location string, line1 int, line2 int, code ErrorCode) *DuplicateKeyError {\n567\t\t// Use provided code or default to duplicate key error\n568\t\terrorCode := code\n569\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n570\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n571\t\t}\n572\t\n573\t\treturn &DuplicateKeyError{\n574\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n575\t\t\tKey:       key,\n576\t\t\tLocation:  location,\n577\t\t\tLine1:     line1,\n578\t\t\tLine2:     line2,\n579\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n580\t\t}\n581\t}\n582\t\n583\t// SchemaLoadError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n595\tfunc NewSchemaLoadError(filePath string, message string, err error, code ErrorCode) *SchemaLoadError {\n596\t\t// Use provided code or &SchemaLoadError{\n603\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n604\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n605\t\t\tErr:       err,\n606\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n607\t\t}\n608\t}\n609\t\n610\t// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n611\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n612\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n613\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n614\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n615\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n616\t\t\t\treturn true\n617\t\t\t}\n618\t\t}\n619\t\treturn false\n620\t}\n621\t\n622\t// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\n623\tfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n624\t\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n625\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n626\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n627\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n628\t\t\t\treturn true\n629\t\t\t}\n630\t\t}\n631\t\treturn false\n632\t}\n633\t\n634\t// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n635\t//\n636\t// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n637\t// information about the file operation that failed.\n638\ttype FileError struct {\n639\t\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n640\t\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n641\t\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n642\t\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n643\t\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n644\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n645\t}\n646\t\n647\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n648\tfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n649\t\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n650\t\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n651\t\t}\n652\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n653\t}\n654\t\n655\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n656\tfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n657\t\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n658\t}\n659\t\n660\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n661\tfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n662\t\top := fe.Operation\n663\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n664\t\t\top = fe.Op\n665\t\t}\n666\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n667\t}\n668\t\n669\t// Error implements the error interface.\n670\tfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n671\t\top := fe.Operation\n672\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n673\t\t\top = fe.Op\n674\t\t}\n675\t\n676\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n677\t\tif op != \"\" {\n678\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s\", op, fe.Path))\n679\t\t} else {\n680\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s\", fe.Path))\n681\t\t}\n682\t\n683\t\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n684\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n685\t\t}\n686\t\n687\t\tif fe.Err != nil {\n688\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n689\t\t}\n690\t\n691\t\treturn sb.String()\n692\t}\n693\t\n694\t// Unwrap being parsed.\n709\ttype SyntaxError struct {\n710\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n711\t\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n712\t\tColumn tme.ActualType)\n848\t}\n849\t\n850\t// FieldNotFoundError represents an error when a required field is missing.\n851\t//\n852\t// FieldNotFoundError is used when accessing required fields that don't {\n1067\t\t\treturn false\n1068\t\t}\n1069\t\n1070\t\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n1071\t\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) \"\"\n1104\t}\n1105\t\n1106\t// IsFileNotFoundError checks if an error indicates a file was not found.\n1107\t// This works with both FileError and other wrapped file not found errors.\n1108\tfunc IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool {\n1109\t\tif err == nil {\n1110\t\t\treturn false\n1111\t\t}\n1112\t\n1113\t\t// Check if error chain contains os.ErrNotExist\n1114\t\tif os.IsNotExist(err)
